title: Wholesome American Chicken Stew
description: Tangy, tender chicken stew perfect for sick days, made without onion or garlic and flavored with fermented jang and ground nut oil.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of ground nut oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the chicken pieces, browning them on all sides for about 5 minutes.
Pour in the remaining tablespoon of ground nut oil and stir to coat the chicken evenly.
Stir in the diced carrots, potatoes, celery, and cook for 2 minutes to release flavors.
Add the chicken broth, apple cider vinegar, fermented-jang, black pepper, and dried thyme, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the stew simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Check the chicken for doneness; it should reach internal temperature of 165°F and be tender.
Taste the stew and adjust seasoning with additional salt or vinegar if needed.
Remove the pot from heat, let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Ladle the stew into bowls, garnish with fresh parsley if desired, and enjoy.
